refined_question: How can graph neural networks effectively leverage graph structure to improve node classification performance in scenarios with limited labeled nodes, without relying on additional labeling?
intent: Understand methods to mitigate overfitting in graph neural networks for node classification tasks with scarce labels, within the domain of graph learning and semi-supervised learning (SSL), focusing on exploiting graph structure
excluded_topics: Using large pre-trained or foundation models as the primary solution
Fine-tuning or transfer learning from large pre-trained/foundation models
Scaling parameter count as the main approach
search_hint: graph neural networks, semi-supervised learning (SSL), node classification, graph structure, limited labeled data, overfitting mitigation, transductive learning, inductive learning, graph attention networks (GAT), graph convolutional networks (GCN)
